cards.back: No. The section sets minimum requirements for retail clients; dealers are not required to provide relationship disclosure to institutional clients.
cards.source_quote: "Dealer Members are not required to provide relationship disclosure to institutional clients." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4687-4688

cards.front: When must relationship disclosure information be provided to a retail client?
cards.back: At the time of opening an account or accounts, and when there is a significant change to the relationship disclosure information previously provided.
cards.source_quote: "(i) at the time of opening an account or accounts, and (ii) when there is a significant change to the relationship disclosure information previously provided to the client." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4695-4697

cards.front: Who approves Relationship Disclosure; standardized-document duty?
cards.back: A partner, Director, officer or designated Supervisor approves, regardless of form; for standardized documents the designated Supervisor ensures the correct document is used per client circumstance.
cards.source_quote: "If the document is a standardized document, the designated Supervisor must ensure that the correct document is used in each client circumstance." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4836-4838

cards.front: The RD's account-relationship description states which three things?
cards.back: Whether the account is advisory, managed or order execution only; whether the client makes investment decisions and how they instruct; whether recommendations or advice will be provided.
cards.source_quote: "whether the account opened is an advisory account, a managed account or an order execution only account" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4743-4744

cards.front: Name the RD's suitability-determination triggering events.
cards.back: Securities received into or delivered out of the account; change in RR/PM/APM responsible; change in client information; change in a security held; the 3209(4) periodic review.
cards.source_quote: "there is a change in the Registered Representative, Portfolio Manager or Associate Portfolio Manager responsible for the account" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4769-4770

cards.front: TRAP: may an OEO dealer omit any Relationship Disclosure content?
cards.back: Only the suitability-process description under 3216(5)(ii)(d), provided disclosure is made in compliance with section 3241. All other RD content still applies.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member does not have to provide the relationship disclosure information required under sub-clause 3216(5)(ii)(d), provided that disclosure is made in compliance with the requirements in section 3241" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4830-4832

cards.front: Three triggers for the leverage risk disclosure statement?
cards.back: Opening a new retail account; prior to an initial recommendation to purchase securities using borrowed money; first becoming aware of the client's intention to purchase with borrowed money.
cards.source_quote: "when first becoming aware of a retail client's intention to purchase securities using borrowed money, a Dealer Member must" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4849-4850

cards.front: When is re-delivery of the leverage risk disclosure statement NOT required?
cards.back: Where the dealer provided the retail client a compliant leverage risk disclosure statement within the last six months.
cards.source_quote: "where it has provided the retail client with a leverage risk disclosure statement in accordance with subsection 3217(1) within the last six months" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 4854-4856

cards.front: What must happen before a margin account is opened?
cards.back: Deliver a margin account agreement to the client and obtain a copy of the agreement signed by the client.
cards.source_quote: "Prior to opening a margin account, a Dealer Member must: (i) deliver a margin account agreement to the client, and (ii) obtain a copy of the margin account agreement signed by the client." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5125-5127

cards.front: What must the dealer ensure when deciding whether to allow a client to trade on margin?
cards.back: That the client is aware of the risks and benefits associated with trading on margin.
cards.source_quote: "In deciding whether to allow a client to trade on margin, a Dealer Member must ensure that the client is aware of the risks and benefits associated with trading on margin." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5122-5123

cards.front: Name the two client OBLIGATIONS the margin account agreement must describe.
cards.back: Pay their indebtedness to the dealer and maintain adequate margin; pay interest on debit balances in their account.
cards.source_quote: "the client's obligation to pay their indebtedness to the Dealer Member and to maintain adequate margin, (ii) the client's obligation to pay interest on debit balances" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5130-5132

cards.front: Which dealer RIGHTS go in the margin account agreement?
cards.back: Raise money on/pledge account assets; use free credit balances; sell assets and cover shorts; use a security against a short sale (incl. dealer/partner/Director accounts); hold assets as collateral.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member's right to raise money on and pledge assets held in the client's account" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5133

cards.front: Client requires prior notice before margin asset sales — what applies?
cards.back: The dealer must set out the nature of the notice and the client's obligations to remedy any deficiency. No fixed regulatory notice period exists.
cards.source_quote: "If the client requires prior notice, the Dealer Member must set out the nature of the notice and the client's obligations to remedy any deficiency" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5137-5138

cards.front: Margin agreement vs letter of undertaking — which one documents a margin account?
cards.back: Only the margin account agreement (3247). The letter of undertaking (3253) substitutes for a DERIVATIVES trading agreement, institutional clients only.
cards.source_quote: "Instead of a derivatives trading agreement, a Dealer Member may obtain a letter of undertaking for accounts where the client is classified as an institutional client." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5268-5269

cards.front: Name the two dealer RIGHTS in 3252(1)(ii) of the derivatives trading agreement.
cards.back: Exercise discretion in accepting orders; impose trading or position limits or closeout positions under specified conditions.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member's right to: (a) exercise discretion in accepting orders, (b) impose trading or position limits or closeout positions under specified conditions" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5196-5198

cards.front: Derivatives agreement: which three dealer rights over client assets?
cards.back: Use free credit balances in its business or to finance other account debits; use account assets as collateral for debit/position obligations; raise money on and pledge account assets.
cards.source_quote: "use client free credit balances within its own business or to finance other client account debits, (b) use client account assets as collateral for the clients' debit and position obligations" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5200-5202

cards.front: Name the three dealer OBLIGATIONS in the derivatives trading agreement.
cards.back: Provide regulators position/exercise-limit information and derivatives data if required; obtain client consent before taking the other side and document whether given; address errors and omissions.
cards.source_quote: "obtain client consent before the Dealer Member may take the other side to the client's transaction, and document whether the client provides such consent" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5211-5212

cards.front: TRAP: does signing the derivatives agreement authorize the dealer to take the other side?
cards.back: No. The dealer must obtain client consent BEFORE taking the other side, and document whether consent is provided.
cards.source_quote: "obtain client consent before the Dealer Member may take the other side to the client's transaction" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5211

cards.front: Discretionary authority given inside the derivatives trading agreement requires what?
cards.back: Disclosures explaining the discretionary authority given, plus the client's acknowledgement of consent — provided the authority is consistent with Part G of Rule 3200.
cards.source_quote: "disclosures explaining the discretionary authority that has been given, (b) the client's acknowledgement that is has consented to the giving of the authority" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5215-5216

cards.front: TRAP: 'address errors and omissions' — dealer right or dealer obligation?
cards.back: Obligation. 3252(1)(v)(c) lists addressing situations when errors and omissions occur among the dealer's obligations, not its discretionary rights.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member's obligation to: (a) if required under any applicable laws or requested to do so, provide information to regulators regarding position limit, exercise limit requirements" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5207-5209

cards.front: Four client OBLIGATIONS in the derivatives trading agreement?
cards.back: Comply with Corporation and trading/clearing/issuing entity requirements (reporting, position, exercise limits); maintain adequate margin collateral, pay debts; pay commission, if any; pay interest, if any, on debits.
cards.source_quote: "maintain adequate margin collateral and to pay any debts owed to the Dealer Member, (c) pay commission or other compensation, if any" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5225-5227

cards.front: Two client ACKNOWLEDGMENTS in the derivatives trading agreement?
cards.back: Receiving the most recent derivatives risk disclosure statement; their duty to inform and update the dealer of circumstances making them an insider of any publicly traded issuer.
cards.source_quote: "the client's acknowledgement of: (a) receiving the most recent derivatives risk disclosure statement" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5229-5230

cards.front: For options and similar derivatives, what notice mechanics must the derivatives agreement set out?
cards.back: The dealer's deadlines for a client to submit an exercise notice; the method the dealer will use to distribute assignment notices.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member's deadlines for a client to submit an exercise notice, (b) the method the Dealer Member will use to distribute assignment notices" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5241-5242

cards.front: Options disclosures 3252(1)(xi)(c)-(d): which four warnings/duties?
cards.back: Dealer may set maximum limits on short positions; may apply cash-only terms the last 10 days before expiry; Corporation may impose other rules; client must instruct close-out before expiry.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member may apply cash-only terms during the last 10 days before expiry" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5245-5246

cards.front: Minimum margin disclosure for futures/forwards/CFDs: greater of which three amounts?
cards.back: The amount the derivatives marketplace or clearing house prescribes; the Corporation's requirements; or the Dealer Member's requirements.
cards.source_quote: "the amount the derivatives marketplace or clearing house prescribes, (b) Corporation's requirements, or (c) the Dealer Member's requirements" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5254-5256

cards.front: Cumulative loss limits apply to accounts transacting in which products?
cards.back: Futures contracts, forward contracts, contracts for difference, futures contract options or similar derivatives, or highly-leveraged securities or derivatives.
cards.source_quote: "transactions involve futures contracts, forward contracts, contracts for difference, futures contract options or similar derivatives, or highly-leveraged securities or derivatives" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5258-5260

cards.front: TRAP: which account is carved OUT of cumulative loss limits, and which account types are IN?
cards.back: Hedging accounts are out. Advisory, discretionary, managed AND order execution only accounts are all in.
cards.source_quote: "applies to an account, other than a hedging account, whether the account is an advisory account, a discretionary account, a managed account or an order execution only account" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5261-5263

cards.front: On what two bases may a cumulative loss limit be determined?
cards.back: A lifetime basis validated with the client on an annual basis, or an annual basis updated annually.
cards.source_quote: "a lifetime basis and validated with the client on an annual basis, or (b) an annual basis and updated annually" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5265-5266

cards.front: Letter of undertaking — what is it; who can use it?
cards.back: An alternative the dealer may obtain instead of a derivatives trading agreement — only where the client is classified as an institutional client.
cards.source_quote: "Instead of a derivatives trading agreement, a Dealer Member may obtain a letter of undertaking for accounts where the client is classified as an institutional client." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5268-5269

cards.front: Two required statements in a letter of undertaking?
cards.back: Client agrees to comply with Corporation requirements, applicable laws and trading/clearing/issuing entity requirements; if charged debit interest, conditions for inter-account transfers of client property (unless acknowledged elsewhere).
cards.source_quote: "if the client has an account that is charged interest on a debit balance, the conditions under which transfers of the client's funds, securities or other property may be made between accounts" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5281-5283

cards.front: List the six requirements to accept managed accounts (3277).
cards.back: Designate a responsible Supervisor; specific P&Ps on supervision/operation; agreement before opening; Supervisor approves each account in writing; retain a record of approval; give client the fair-allocation policy.
cards.source_quote: "the designated Supervisor must approve each managed account in writing, (v) the Dealer Member must retain a record of the designated Supervisor's approval" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5416-5417

cards.front: Which managed-account policy must be handed TO the client?
cards.back: A copy of the dealer's policy ensuring fair allocation of investment opportunities.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member must provide the client with a copy of its policy ensuring fair allocation of investment opportunities" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5418-5419

cards.front: What three things must a managed account agreement contain?
cards.back: Describe/refer to the client's personal-financial circumstances, knowledge, time horizon, needs/objectives, risk profile; describe client-imposed restrictions where permitted; set out 3278(2) termination terms.
cards.source_quote: "describe or refer to the client's personal and financial circumstances, investment knowledge, investment time horizon, investment needs and objectives and risk profile" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5422-5423

cards.front: Managed account termination: each party's written notice effective when?
cards.back: Client: on receipt by the dealer, except transactions entered before receipt. Dealer: not less than 30 days from the date the dealer delivered the notice.
cards.source_quote: "by the Dealer Member, effective not less than 30 days from the date the Dealer Member delivered the notice to the client" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5431-5432

cards.front: TRAP: client terminates a managed account; the PM traded that morning. Do trades stand?
cards.back: Yes. Client termination is effective on receipt, EXCEPT for transactions entered prior to receipt of the notice.
cards.source_quote: "by the client, effective on receipt by the Dealer Member, except for transactions entered prior to receipt of the notice" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5429-5430

cards.front: What does 3280(1) prohibit around managed-account trade information?
cards.back: A responsible person or dealer must not trade their own account — or knowingly permit any associate/affiliate to trade — relying on information about trades in a managed account.
cards.source_quote: "must not trade for their or the Dealer Member's own account, or knowingly permit or arrange any associate or affiliate to trade, in reliance upon information relating to trades made or to be made in a managed account" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5448-5450

cards.front: Which three managed-account investments need PRIOR WRITTEN CONSENT?
cards.back: Issuers related/connected to a responsible person or the dealer; issuers where the authorized individual is an officer/director (unless disclosed); new issues or secondary offerings underwritten by the dealer.
cards.source_quote: "invest in a security or derivative of a security of an issuer that is related or connected to a responsible person or to the Dealer Member" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5457-5458

cards.front: Which three managed-account actions are banned OUTRIGHT — no consent cure?
cards.back: Buying/selling from or to a PM's, APM's or associate's account; from or to a fund a responsible person advises; guarantees or loans to a responsible person/associate.
cards.source_quote: "purchase or sell a security or derivative of a security of an issuer from or to an investment fund for which a responsible person acts as an adviser" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5467-5468

cards.front: TRAP: PM sells from a managed account to her own account, client consents in writing. Permitted?
cards.back: No. Written consent cures only 3280(2) investments; principal trades with PM/APM/associate accounts fall under 3280(3) — no consent exception.
cards.source_quote: "must not knowingly cause any managed account to: (i) purchase or sell a security or derivative of a security of an issuer from or to the account of a Portfolio Manager" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5463-5465

cards.front: How must a dealer allocate investment opportunities among managed accounts?
cards.back: Fairly — the dealer must fairly allocate investment opportunities among its managed accounts.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member must fairly allocate investment opportunities among its managed accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5470

cards.front: Volume/performance fees to clients vs transaction-based PM compensation — which has a cure?
cards.back: Direct client charges (3281(1)): permitted with a written agreement setting out how fees may be charged. Compensating 3279 persons by transaction value/volume (3281(2)): no cure.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member must not compensate a person referred to in section 3279, on the basis of the value or volume of transactions in the account." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5479-5480

cards.front: Which two direct managed-account fee types need the written agreement?
cards.back: Fees based upon the volume or value of transactions initiated for the account; fees contingent upon profit or performance of the client's account.
cards.source_quote: "based upon the volume or value of transactions in the account initiated for the account, or (ii) contingent upon profit or performance of the client's account" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5474-5475

cards.front: TRAP: may the discretionary-accounts designated Supervisor delegate the monthly financial performance reviews?
cards.back: No — must not delegate the performance of the 3970(1)-(2) reviews to ANY other person. This overrides the general delegate-tasks framework.
cards.source_quote: "The designated Supervisor responsible for discretionary accounts must not delegate the performance of the reviews required in subsections 3970(1) and 3970(2) to any other person." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 8384-8385

cards.front: List the six requirements to accept discretionary accounts (3273).
cards.back: Designate Supervisor(s) meeting Rule 2600 proficiency; specific P&Ps per Rule 3900; identify accounts in books and records; agreement before acceptance; Supervisor approves account and signed agreement; retain approval record.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member must designate one or more designated Supervisors, who meet the proficiency requirements set out in Rule 2600, to be responsible for the discretionary accounts" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5352-5354

cards.front: Discretionary sequencing: when must the agreement exist; what gets approved?
cards.back: Agreement entered into prior to accepting the account as discretionary; designated Supervisor approves the account as discretionary AND the agreement signed by the client.
cards.source_quote: "the designated Supervisor must approve the account as a discretionary account and approve the discretionary account agreement signed by the client" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5361-5362

cards.front: What experience qualifies an RR to effect discretionary trades?
cards.back: At least two years of active experience trading, advising or performing analysis on all product types to be traded on a discretionary basis.
cards.source_quote: "the Registered Representative has at least two years of active experience in trading, advising or performing analysis with respect to all types of products that are to be traded on a discretionary basis" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5383-5385

cards.front: TRAP: Rule 2600 proficiency vs PM approval — what qualifies the discretionary-accounts Supervisor?
cards.back: Rule 2600 proficiency requirements (3273(1)(i)). PM approval is a separate category — it matters for trade-by-trade pre-review exemptions, not Supervisor designation.
cards.source_quote: "who meet the proficiency requirements set out in Rule 2600, to be responsible for the discretionary accounts" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5352-5354

cards.front: What are the term and renewal limits of a discretionary account agreement?
cards.back: Maximum term of no longer than 12 months, and the agreement must not be renewable.
cards.source_quote: "have a maximum term of no longer than 12 months, (iv) not be renewable" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5373-5374

cards.front: TRAP: do discretionary and managed accounts differ on termination-notice mechanics?
cards.back: No — both: client notice effective on receipt (prior transactions stand); dealer notice effective not less than 30 days from delivery. Only discretionary accounts carry the 12-month non-renewable cap.
cards.source_quote: "by the Dealer Member, effective not less than 30 days from the date the Dealer Member delivered the notice to the client" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5379-5380
cards.qa_note: Reworked 2026-06-04 from near-duplicate of FC-E4-0053 into a cross-account distinction card.

cards.front: TRAP: discretionary account holds dealer shares with client consent — OK?
cards.back: Never — it must not hold any publicly traded securities of the Dealer Member or its affiliates. The 3276(3) consent cure does not reach this.
cards.source_quote: "A discretionary account must not hold any publicly traded securities of the Dealer Member or its affiliates." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5389-5390

cards.front: Discretionary accounts: which investments need prior written consent; what is flatly banned?
cards.back: Consent: issuers where a 3275(1) authorized individual is an officer/director (position disclosed); dealer-underwritten new issues/secondary offerings. Banned: guarantees or loans to a responsible person/associate.
cards.source_quote: "must not allow a discretionary account to provide a guarantee or loan to a responsible person or an associate of a responsible person" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5400-5401

cards.front: Discretionary vs managed account — which definitional features separate them?
cards.back: Discretionary: unsolicited authority, accommodates a frequently/temporarily unavailable client, not renewed, term ≤12 months. Managed: continuing investment decisions by a PM/APM or hired third party.
cards.source_quote: "the discretionary authority is accepted to accommodate a client who is frequently or temporarily unavailable to authorize trades" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 349-350

cards.front: List the four steps before an INITIAL derivatives transaction in any account type.
cards.back: Completed derivatives account application; completed, signed derivatives trading agreement; provide the most recent derivatives disclosure statement (or similar); record the relevant designated Supervisors' approval in writing.
cards.source_quote: "obtain a completed derivatives account application from the client, (ii) obtain a completed and signed derivatives trading agreement from the client" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5174-5175

cards.front: Intended derivatives strategies don't fit the client. The designated Supervisors should:
cards.back: Restrict the account from inappropriate strategies, note trading restrictions on the derivatives account approval, and communicate them to the assigned RR/PM/APM.
cards.source_quote: "the designated Supervisors should restrict the account from using inappropriate strategies and note on the derivatives account approval any trading restrictions imposed" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5182-5184

cards.front: What standard governs assessment of a client's intended derivatives strategies?
cards.back: Strategies' risk characteristics must be appropriate and in keeping with personal/financial circumstances, needs and objectives, knowledge, risk profile, time horizon — putting the client's interest first.
cards.source_quote: "determine whether the risk characteristics of the strategies the client intends to use are appropriate for the client and in keeping with their personal and financial circumstances" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5179-5181

cards.front: Appropriateness determination vs designated-Supervisor account approval — when does each occur?
cards.back: Appropriateness (3211): BEFORE opening the account. Designated Supervisor approval (3214): up to one business day AFTER the initial trade. Distractors swap the timings.
cards.source_quote: "Before a Dealer Member opens an account for a person, the Dealer Member must determine" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4573

cards.front: Which two investor signals make an OEO account inappropriate (4.21)?
cards.back: Incapable of conducting online activities (e.g., clear and persistent difficulties completing the online new-account application form); the investor is looking for advice.
cards.source_quote: "If a person would be incapable of conducting online activities (e.g. clear and persistent difficulties in completing the OEO Investment Dealer's online new-account application form)" — syllabus-raw.txt ~lines 1046-1048

cards.front: OEO derivative account approval restrictions cover which products/strategies?
cards.back: OTC derivatives; options strategies other than low-risk strategies based on long calls, long puts, covered calls or protective (long) puts; trading in CFDs; futures or similar derivative trading strategies.
cards.source_quote: "Options trading strategies other than low-risk strategies based on long calls, long puts, covered calls or protective (long) puts" — syllabus-raw.txt ~lines 1071-1072

cards.front: Name the three written OEO disclosures required before opening.
cards.back: No recommendations — client solely responsible for all investment decisions; no 3402/3403 suitability determination; no determination that products and account types offered are appropriate.
cards.source_quote: "a statement confirming that the Dealer Member will not provide any recommendations to the client and that the client is solely responsible for making all investment decisions" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5001-5003

cards.front: Who acknowledges the OEO disclosures, and in what recorded forms?
cards.back: The client AND each beneficial owner. Forms: signature/initials on a document specific to the disclosure; electronic acknowledgement attached to the text; or taped verbal acknowledgement.
cards.source_quote: "obtain a positive acknowledgement from the client, and each beneficial owner of the account, confirming that the client, and each beneficial owner, has received and understood the disclosures" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5014-5016

cards.front: TRAP: who keeps the managed-account approval record, and who receives the fair-allocation policy?
cards.back: The dealer retains the designated Supervisor's written approval record; the CLIENT receives the fair-allocation policy copy. Distractors swap recipient and retainer.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member must retain a record of the designated Supervisor's approval, and (vi) the Dealer Member must provide the client with a copy of its policy" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5417-5418

cards.front: Delivering the leverage risk disclosure statement is not enough — what else is required?
cards.back: The retail client's positive acknowledgement that they received it.
cards.source_quote: "obtain the retail client's positive acknowledgement that they are in receipt of the" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4852

cards.front: First-tier vs second-tier supervision: what is the second tier designed to do?
cards.back: Ensure no serious account issues were missed by first-tier supervision, and ensure first-tier supervision is conducted adequately. Different purpose; generally not the same depth.
cards.source_quote: "ensure no serious account issues were missed by ﬁrst-tier supervision, and ensure ﬁrst-tier supervision is conducted adequately." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 266

cards.front: When may first-tier reviews be conducted centrally (head/regional office) instead of at each branch?
cards.back: If resources are available to conduct the review at that location AND the Dealer has adequate procedures for dealing with any issues identified.
cards.source_quote: "Dealers can conduct these reviews in a central location (head oDce or regional oDce) provided: there are resources available to conduct the review at that location," — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 229

cards.front: Remote supervision: what control applies to a business location without a Resident Supervisor?
cards.back: An assigned outside Supervisor who visits periodically to review business conduct and legal compliance; P&Ps must specifically address those visits.
cards.source_quote: "Business locations without Resident Supervisors should have an assigned outside Supervisor. The outside Supervisor should visit the location periodically to review business conduct and compliance with applicable laws." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 169

cards.front: Account supervision P&Ps must specifically address which three dealer obligations (3926(2))?
cards.back: Identify clients presenting high risk to the Dealer Member; identify clients presenting high risk of improper securities-market activities; comply with all AML and terrorist financing requirements.
cards.source_quote: "(i) identify clients that present a high risk to the Dealer Member, (ii) identify clients that present a high risk of conducting improper activities in the securities markets" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8099

cards.front: Per CIRO guidance, which client profiles present a higher risk of improper market activity?
cards.back: Those with access to material non-public information about issuers; holders of control blocks of public issuers; market professionals.
cards.source_quote: "such as those who have access to material non-public information about issuers, holders of control blocks of public issuers and market professionals." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 200

cards.front: What must a dealer's P&Ps include regarding client records (3926(5))?
cards.back: Controls for accessing and amending client records — untracked, unrestricted editing of KYC/client data breaches this.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member's policies and procedures must include controls for accessing and amending client records." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8111

cards.front: Who approves account supervision P&Ps and any substantive amendments?
cards.back: The Dealer Member's Chief Compliance Officer or another appropriate Executive — not CIRO, not the branch Supervisor.
cards.source_quote: "any substantive amendments to such policies and procedures, must be approved by the Dealer Member's Chief Compliance Officer or another appropriate Executive." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8105

cards.front: What two written items must a dealer provide all supervisory staff (3926(4))?
cards.back: Procedures to be followed in reviewing account activity; confirmation of the dealer's expectations of supervisory staff regarding their supervisory roles and responsibilities.
cards.source_quote: "(i) procedures to be followed in reviewing account activity, and (ii) confirmation of the Dealer Member's expectations of supervisory staff," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8108

cards.front: What does IDPC 3927(3) require for Approved Persons with a history of regulatory infractions or questionable conduct?
cards.back: The dealer must establish and follow procedures implementing additional supervisory measures applicable to those Approved Persons — prior discipline increases, not discharges, supervision.
cards.source_quote: "procedures for the implementation of additional supervisory measures applicable to Approved Persons with a history of regulatory infractions or questionable conduct." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8131

cards.front: Name the two strict (enhanced) supervisory measures the syllabus lists for infraction-history Approved Persons.
cards.back: Pre-approval of orders by the Supervisor; daily and monthly review of ALL the AP's client accounts.
cards.source_quote: "Pre-approval of orders by Supervisor • Daily and monthly review of all client accounts" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1110

cards.front: What specific OEO risk must trade-review P&Ps address under 3955(2)?
cards.back: The risks associated with the method of order entry and the absence of intermediation by employees of the Dealer Member.
cards.source_quote: "must specifically address the risks associated with the method of order entry and the absence of intermediation by employees of the Dealer Member." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8267

cards.front: What supervision records and resources must the OEO entity maintain (3955(3)-(4))?
cards.back: An audit trail of all supervisory reviews as required in Rule 3900; sufficient supervisory resources allocated at head office and branch levels.
cards.source_quote: "must maintain an audit trail of all supervisory reviews as required in Rule 3900." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8269

cards.front: Two conditions before placing a temporary hold for financial exploitation of a vulnerable client?
cards.back: Dealer reasonably believes BOTH: the client is a vulnerable client, AND financial exploitation has occurred, is occurring, has been attempted or will be attempted.
cards.source_quote: "must not place a temporary hold on the basis of financial exploitation of a vulnerable client, unless the Dealer Member reasonably believes all of the following: (i) the client is a vulnerable client," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4925

cards.front: After placing a temporary hold, what must the dealer do every 30 days until it ends?
cards.back: Within 30 days and each subsequent 30-day period: either revoke the hold, or give the client notice of the decision to continue it plus reasons.
cards.source_quote: "within 30 days of placing the temporary hold and, until the hold is revoked, within every subsequent 30‐day period," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4947

cards.front: TRAP: temporary hold for lack of mental capacity — does it require a doctor's certificate?
cards.back: No. Standard is the dealer's reasonable belief the client lacks mental capacity to make decisions involving financial matters — a separate ground from exploitation.
cards.source_quote: "unless the Dealer Member reasonably believes that the client does not have the mental capacity to make decisions involving financial matters." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4937

cards.front: 3945(2) retail detection list — recite the first six items (suitability/conduct cluster).
cards.back: Unsuitable trading; undue concentration (single account or across accounts); excessive trading; restricted-securities/derivatives trading; AP-client trading conflict of interest; excessive transfers and cancellations indicating possible unauthorized trading.
cards.source_quote: "(i) unsuitable trading, (ii) undue concentration of securities, derivatives or precious metals bullion in a single account or across accounts, (iii) excessive trading," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8145

cards.front: 3945(2) retail detection list — items (vii)-(xi) (pattern/conduct cluster)?
cards.back: Inappropriate or high-risk trading strategies; deterioration of quality of client holdings; excessive or improper crosses between clients; improper or excessive employee trading; front running.
cards.source_quote: "(vii) inappropriate or high risk trading strategies, (viii) deterioration of the quality of client holdings in an account," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8155

cards.front: 3945(2) retail detection list — final six items (administrative/market-abuse cluster)?
cards.back: Account number changes; late payment; outstanding margin calls; undisclosed short sales; manipulative and deceptive activities; insider trading.
cards.source_quote: "(xii) account number changes, (xiii) late payment, (xiv) outstanding margin calls, (xv) undisclosed short sales, (xvi) manipulative and deceptive activities, and" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8161

cards.front: Daily review red flag: what pattern signals possible UNAUTHORIZED trading?
cards.back: Excessive transaction transfers and trade cancellations — moving or unwinding trades hides activity the client never authorized.
cards.source_quote: "excessive transaction transfers and trade cancellations indicating possible unauthorized trading," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8153

cards.front: Churning (excessive trading): why won't single-trade review catch it, and what does guidance prescribe?
cards.back: Patterns may not be apparent in single trades; reviewing trades over a longer period can detect overall-activity issues even if each trade is suitable.
cards.source_quote: "If a Dealer reviews trades over a longer period, they may detect issues with the overall account activity even if each trade is suitable for the client." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 191

cards.front: TRAP: fee-based retail accounts — can review selection be based on commission levels?
cards.back: No. P&Ps for accounts where no commission is charged must address review requirements AND use criteria other than commission levels.
cards.source_quote: "such as fee based accounts. These policies and procedures must: (i) address account activity review Corporation requirements, and (ii) use criteria other than commission levels." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8176

cards.front: Which five retail account types must be specifically designated for supervision purposes (3945(4))?
cards.back: Non-client accounts; discretionary accounts; managed accounts; registered accounts; restricted accounts.
cards.source_quote: "(i) non-client accounts, (ii) discretionary accounts, (iii) managed accounts, (iv) registered accounts, and (v) restricted accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8182

cards.front: Expected completion timing: first-tier DAILY review vs first-tier MONTHLY review?
cards.back: Daily: one business day after the trading activity occurs. Monthly: within 21 days of the review period — both unless precluded by unusual circumstances.
cards.source_quote: "Dealers should complete this review within 21 days of the review period, unless precluded by unusual circumstances." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 254

cards.front: TRAP: which non-client accounts go into the first-tier MONTHLY review?
cards.back: ALL non-client accounts with any activity other than dividend and interest payments — not just high-commission ones. ($3,000 belongs to second-tier monthly client criteria.)
cards.source_quote: "A ﬁrst-tier monthly review should include all non-client accounts with any activity other than dividend and interest payments." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 251

cards.front: Guidance example selection thresholds: first-tier monthly vs second-tier monthly?
cards.back: First-tier monthly: clients charged gross commissions of $1,500+ that month. Second-tier monthly: clients charged $3,000+, plus $1,500+ accounts not subject to first-tier review.
cards.source_quote: "a Dealer could review the activity of all clients charged gross commissions of $1,500 or more for that month." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 249

cards.front: Beyond trades, which four client-related matters must P&Ps identify, deal with, and inform Supervisors of (3946(1))?
cards.back: Client complaints; cash account violations; transfers between unrelated accounts or between non-client and client accounts (incl. deposits); trading while under-margined.
cards.source_quote: "(i) client complaints, (ii) cash account violations, (iii) transfers of funds and positions between unrelated accounts or between non-client accounts and client accounts" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8191

cards.front: Name six example second-tier DAILY review criteria from CIRO guidance.
cards.back: Stock trades >$5,000 priced under $5.00; stock trades >$20,000 at/over $5.00; bond trades >$100,000; non-client trading; producing branch managers' client accounts; trade cancellations.
cards.source_quote: "stock trades with a value over $5,000 and a price under $5.00 per share, stock trades with value over $20,000 and a price at or over $5.00 per share," — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 279

cards.front: Retail debt trade (non-primary-market): what remuneration disclosure must the confirmation contain?
cards.back: Either the total mark-up/mark-down, commission or other service charges applied, or total commission plus prescribed notice that dealer remuneration was added/deducted.
cards.source_quote: "the total amount of any mark-up or mark-down, commission or other service charges the Dealer Member applied to the transaction," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 7609

cards.front: Cross-supervision: a dually-registered Supervisor also trades for their own clients. Who reviews their client activity?
cards.back: Another Supervisor must review that Supervisor's advice to and trades for clients — no one supervises themselves.
cards.source_quote: "where a Supervisors is dually-registered and also advises or trades for their own clients, another Supervisor reviews that Supervisor's advice to and trades for clients." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 148

cards.front: Which second-tier daily criterion plugs the producing-branch-manager self-review gap?
cards.back: 'Producing branch managers' client accounts' (plus all client accounts not reviewed by a branch manager) — the second tier catches what the producing manager cannot independently review.
cards.source_quote: "producing branch managers' client accounts, all client accounts not reviewed by a branch manager," — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 283

cards.front: Hold-mail procedures for retail clients — three minimum provisions (3219(1))?
cards.back: Written client authorization to hold mail; time limit — no longer than six months in any 12-month period; control and regular review of hold-mail accounts by a Supervisor.
cards.source_quote: "a requirement that limits the length of time that a "hold mail" order may remain in force for no longer than six months, in any 12 month period," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4883

cards.front: TRAP: can a hold-mail order ever exceed six months?
cards.back: Yes, if: permitted by the dealer's P&Ps; P&Ps specifically address close supervision of such accounts; AND the appropriate Supervisor pre-approves the extended period.
cards.source_quote: "(ii) the Dealer Member has policies and procedures that specifically address the close supervision of such accounts, and (iii) the appropriate Supervisor pre‐approves the extended period." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4892

cards.front: Returned mail for retail clients: two minimum procedural rules (3219(3))?
cards.back: Control and investigation by a person independent of the sales function (may sit in a business location); a record of all investigations and their results maintained.
cards.source_quote: "a rule requiring the control and investigation by a person independent of the sales function, but may be located within a business location," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4897

cards.front: What must institutional account supervision P&Ps outline besides detection (3950(1))?
cards.back: The actions to deal with problems or issues identified from supervisory reviews — detection alone is not enough; remediation must be specified.
cards.source_quote: "These policies and procedures must outline the actions to deal with problems or issues identified from supervisory reviews." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8225

cards.front: 3950(2) institutional detection list — recite all seven items.
cards.back: Manipulative/deceptive activities; securities on the restricted list; derivatives with restricted-list underlying; front running by employee/proprietary accounts; transfer-restricted securities; derivatives with transfer-restricted underlying; exceeding derivative position/exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "(i) manipulative and deceptive activities, (ii) trading in securities on the Dealer Member's restricted list," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8231

cards.front: Institutional front-running detection (3950(2)(iv)): whose accounts does the rule target?
cards.back: Front running by EMPLOYEE or PROPRIETARY accounts — trading ahead of institutional client orders through the firm's own or staff accounts.
cards.source_quote: "front running by employee or proprietary accounts," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8235

cards.front: TRAP: is 'unsuitable trading' or 'churning' on the institutional 3950(2) detection list?
cards.back: No — those are retail 3945(2) items. The institutional list is market-integrity focused: manipulation, restricted lists, front running, transfer restrictions, position/exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "specifically address detecting improper or suspicious account activity including: (i) manipulative and deceptive activities," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8230

cards.front: A second-tier reviewer of institutional activity lacks authority to act on findings. What must procedures provide?
cards.back: Procedures for referring the identified issues to a Supervisor who has the appropriate authority.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer should have procedures for referring these issues to a Supervisor who has the appropriate authority." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 270

cards.front: TRAP: 'trading activity around margin/reduced margin rates and price thresholds' appears in which detection list?
cards.back: Syllabus 5.9's INSTITUTIONAL improper-activity list — a syllabus-only addition beyond the seven 3950(2) rule items.
cards.source_quote: "Trading activity around margin/reduced margin rates and price thresholds" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1178

cards.front: Define a 'direct electronic access account' (IDPC 1201).
cards.back: Account not subject to suitability determination where: client has DEA per NI 23-103; dealer provides no recommendations; dealer complies with UMIR DEA requirements and NI 23-103.
cards.source_quote: "the client has been provided with direct electronic access within the meaning of National Instrument 23-103," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 334

cards.front: DEA accounts and suitability sections 3402/3403 — fully exempt, or not quite?
cards.back: Almost — exempt except clauses 3402(3)(i) and 3403(4)(i): the determination that CONTINUING to have the account is suitable still applies.
cards.source_quote: "Other than clauses 3402(3)(i) and 3403(4)(i), sections 3402 or 3403 do not apply in respect to: (i) an order execution only account, or (ii) a direct electronic access account." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5643

cards.front: OEO P&Ps: a client also has a full-service account at the dealer. May the OEO unit give recommendations?
cards.back: No — P&Ps must ensure clients are not provided recommendations as a result of having an account with a separate legal entity, separate business unit, or the dealer itself.
cards.source_quote: "ensure that clients are not provided with recommendations as a result of the client having an account with: (a) a separate legal entity of the Dealer Member," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8252

cards.front: OEO accounts are exempt from suitability — so what trading review scope still applies?
cards.back: Review customer trading and accounts for ALL Rule 3900 concerns other than those relating to the suitability requirements (manipulation, restricted lists, margin, etc.).
cards.source_quote: "to review customer trading and accounts for those concerns listed in Rule 3900, other than those relating to the suitability requirements." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8257

cards.front: OEO accounts offering OTC derivatives (e.g., CFDs) to retail clients: what extra disclosure accompanies the risk disclosure statement?
cards.back: The percentage of such accounts that were profitable for each of the four most recent quarters.
cards.source_quote: "a disclosure of the percentage of such accounts that were profitable for each of the four most recent quarters." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5298

cards.front: A dealer transacts in both options and futures/CFDs. How must designated derivatives supervision be structured (3960(1))?
cards.back: Appoint, where applicable: a designated Supervisor for options contracts or similar derivatives, AND one for futures, forwards, CFDs, futures contract options or similar derivatives.
cards.source_quote: "appoint a designated Supervisor to supervise its activities that involve futures contracts, forward contracts, contracts for difference, futures contract options or similar derivatives." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8281

cards.front: TRAP: designated vs ALTERNATE derivatives Supervisor — when must the alternate step in (3960(3)-(4))?
cards.back: Alternates are appointed to ensure CONTINUOUS supervision; they must assume duties when the designated Supervisor is absent/unable, or when trading activity needs additional qualified supervisors.
cards.source_quote: "(i) the relevant designated Supervisor is absent or unable to carry out their duties, or (ii) a Dealer Member's trading activity requires additional qualified individuals" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8288

cards.front: Designated derivatives Supervisors are responsible for which two things (3961)?
cards.back: Approving new derivatives accounts; ensuring the handling of clients' derivatives account trading complies with Corporation requirements.
cards.source_quote: "(i) approving new derivatives accounts, and (ii) ensuring that the handling of clients' derivatives account trading complies with Corporation requirements." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8293

cards.front: 3963(1) retail derivatives detection — first five items (margin/limits cluster)?
cards.back: Excessive intra-day and short-term transactions; transacting while under-margined; transacting beyond margin or credit limits; cumulative losses exceeding approved client loss limits; exceeding derivative position or exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "(i) excessive intra-day and short-term transactions, (ii) transacting while the account is under-margined, (iii) transacting beyond margin or credit limits," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8341

cards.front: 3963(1) retail derivatives detection — items (vi)-(xi) (conduct/exposure cluster)?
cards.back: Speculative transactions in hedging accounts; derivatives with restricted-list underlying; underlying with transfer restrictions; derivatives used to avoid insider trading restrictions; uncovered option exposures; futures/forwards held into the delivery month.
cards.source_quote: "(vi) speculative transactions in hedging accounts, (vii) transactions in derivatives whose underlying interest is on the Dealer Member's restricted list," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8347

cards.front: TRAP: 3950(2) institutional list vs 3963(1) retail derivatives list — where do 'speculative transactions in hedging accounts' and 'uncovered option exposures' belong?
cards.back: Both are 3963(1) RETAIL derivatives items only. Shared with the institutional list: restricted-list derivatives, transfer-restricted underlyings, position/exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "(x) exposures arising out of uncovered option positions, and" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8359

cards.front: TRAP: which derivatives account type is carved out of the cumulative loss-limit regime?
cards.back: Hedging accounts. The limit otherwise applies whether the account is advisory, discretionary, managed or order execution only.
cards.source_quote: "applies to an account, other than a hedging account, whether the account is an advisory account, a discretionary account, a managed account or an order execution only account," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5261

cards.front: How is a client's cumulative loss limit determined and maintained (3252(2)(iii))?
cards.back: Either on a lifetime basis validated with the client annually, or on an annual basis updated annually.
cards.source_quote: "(a) a lifetime basis and validated with the client on an annual basis, or (b) an annual basis and updated annually." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5265

cards.front: TRAP: must every retail derivatives account be reviewed daily? Which ones MUST be reviewed daily AND monthly?
cards.back: No — accounts are selected using criteria giving reasonable assurance of detecting improper trading. But the designated Supervisor must review ALL derivatives discretionary and managed accounts daily and monthly.
cards.source_quote: "On a daily and monthly basis, the designated Supervisor must review all derivatives accounts that are designated as discretionary accounts and managed accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8314

cards.front: Retail derivatives client notifications — P&Ps must address notifying clients of which four events (3962(4))?
cards.back: Approaching expiry dates; significant changes in derivatives from changes in the underlying interest; changes in the dealer's business policy; new developments in derivatives trading or regulation impacting clients.
cards.source_quote: "(i) approaching expiry dates, (ii) significant changes in derivatives resulting from changes in the underlying interest, (iii) any changes in the Dealer Member's business policy," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8318

cards.front: Retail CFD float cap: what intra-day/short-term position size must P&Ps prevent?
cards.back: A retail client holding CFD or similar derivative positions representing more than 0.5% of the float of a publicly traded issuer, on an intra-day or short-term basis.
cards.source_quote: "prevent a retail client from holding contracts for difference or similar derivatives positions representing more than 0.5% of the float of a reporting issuer" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8331

cards.front: TRAP: 0.5%-of-float cap vs the CFD product prohibition — which clause bans WHAT?
cards.back: 3962(5)(v) caps position SIZE at 0.5% of float. 3962(5)(vi) categorically prohibits retail CFDs conferring rights/obligations to acquire or deliver the underlying or shareholder rights (e.g., voting).
cards.source_quote: "prohibit the offering of contracts for difference or similar derivatives to a retail client that confer the right or obligation to acquire or deliver the underlying interest" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8334

cards.front: Before soliciting clients to use derivatives programs, what approval do P&Ps require? And before any transaction?
cards.back: Designated Supervisor must approve solicitation of clients to use derivatives programs AND clients' actual use of derivatives; no transacting without an executed derivatives trading agreement.
cards.source_quote: "require the designated Supervisor to approve the solicitation of clients to use derivatives programs, as well as clients' actual use of derivatives," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8323

cards.front: Besides solicitation approval and the trading agreement, name the other two 'address' items in 3962(5).
cards.back: Handling of futures, forwards and similar derivatives with pending delivery months; detection of derivatives trading by an insider client to avoid insider trading restrictions.
cards.source_quote: "address detection of derivatives trading by a client who is an insider of a reporting issuer or any other issuer whose securities are publicly traded to avoid insider trading restrictions," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8329

cards.front: What access must derivatives clients have during normal business hours (3964)?
cards.back: Access to an RR, IR, PM or APM qualified to deal in the applicable derivatives — options-type, futures/forwards/CFDs-type, or all derivatives.
cards.source_quote: "derivatives clients have access, during normal business hours, to a Registered Representative, Investment Representative, Portfolio Manager or Associate Portfolio Manager qualified to deal in," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8365

cards.front: Managed account supervision P&Ps must specifically address which two matters (3971(2))?
cards.back: Identifying when a PM or sub-adviser has contravened the section 3280 managed-account conflict-of-interest requirements; ensuring fairness in allocating investment opportunities among managed accounts.
cards.source_quote: "ensuring fairness in the allocation of investment opportunities among its managed accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8416

cards.front: TRAP: Associate Portfolio Manager advice to managed accounts — pre-approval or next-day review?
cards.back: PRE-approval: the APM is prohibited from providing advice unless a Portfolio Manager at the dealer approved it BEFORE the APM provides it. (Next-day review belongs to discretionary orders.)
cards.source_quote: "including a prohibition on the Associate Portfolio Manager providing advice unless the advice has been approved by a Portfolio Manager at the Dealer Member prior" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8420

cards.front: Managed account committee: minimum composition and annual duties (3972)?
cards.back: Includes at least one designated Supervisor responsible for managed accounts plus the CCO; at least annually reviews managed-account supervision P&Ps and recommends compliance actions to senior management.
cards.source_quote: "a managed account committee that includes at least one designated Supervisor responsible for managed accounts and the Chief Compliance Officer. The committee must, at least annually:" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8430

cards.front: TRAP: which supervisory reviews can the designated Supervisor NOT delegate, and how often is each managed account reviewed?
cards.back: Discretionary-account monthly financial-performance reviews (3970(1)-(2)) cannot be delegated to any other person; each managed account is reviewed QUARTERLY (objectives pursued; compliant handling).
cards.source_quote: "must not delegate the performance of the reviews required in subsections 3970(1) and 3970(2) to any other person." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8384

cards.front: A Supervisor resolves a flagged trade by phone and records nothing. What does 3927(2) require?
cards.back: Record and keep evidence of completed supervisory reviews, including details of inquiries about issues and their resolution, for the section 3803 retention period.
cards.source_quote: "must record and keep evidence of completed supervisory reviews, including details of inquiries about issues and their resolution, for the period required in section 3803." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8129

cards.front: Default retention period for dealer records, including supervision documentation?
cards.back: Minimum seven years from the date the record is created, in durable and accessible form, unless a specific requirement prescribes differently.
cards.source_quote: "in durable and accessible form, for a minimum of seven years from the date the record is created unless Corporation requirements or securities laws" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 6721

cards.front: How often must a Participant's automated order systems be tested?
cards.back: Before use and at least annually thereafter, assuming various market conditions.
cards.source_quote: "and at least annually thereafter, each automated order system is satisfactorily tested assuming" — umir.txt ~line 7005

cards.front: Besides testing, what real-time controls must automated order systems have?
cards.back: Pre-programmed internal parameters that prevent or flag, in real time, orders/trades exceeding volume, order, price or other limits.
cards.source_quote: "internal parameters to prevent or “flag” with alerts on a real-time basis, the entry of orders and" — umir.txt ~line 7008

cards.front: What 'kill switch' capability does UMIR Policy 7.1 require for automated order systems?
cards.back: The Participant or Access Person must be able to immediately override or automatically disable the system, preventing its orders reaching any marketplace.
cards.source_quote: "Each Participant or Access Person must have the ability to immediately override or disable" — umir.txt ~line 7011

cards.front: TRAP: may OEO clients connect their own automated order systems?
cards.back: No — OEO clients must not use their own automated order system to generate/send orders or send orders on a pre-determined basis.
cards.source_quote: "use their own automated order system, as defined in securities laws, to generate" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4986

cards.front: Whose risk controls govern a DEA client's orders sent to a marketplace?
cards.back: The Participant's: every order remains subject to its risk management and supervisory controls, policies and procedures, including automated controls.
cards.source_quote: "the order is subject to the risk management and supervisory controls, policies and procedures established by the Participant including the automated controls to examine each order before entry on a marketplace." — umir.txt ~line 8927

cards.front: What may an Investment Representative do — and NOT do — with securities or derivatives?
cards.back: Trade in, but not advise on, securities or derivatives on the Dealer Member's behalf — including where dealing only in mutual funds.
cards.source_quote: "An individual, approved by the Corporation, to trade in, but not advise on, securities or derivatives, on the Dealer Member's behalf" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 503

cards.front: How does Rule 1201 distinguish a Portfolio Manager from an Associate Portfolio Manager?
cards.back: Both provide discretionary portfolio management for managed accounts; the APM does so under the supervision of a Portfolio Manager.
cards.source_quote: "to provide discretionary portfolio management for managed accounts under the supervision of a Portfolio Manager." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 208

cards.front: TRAP: An APM gives advice while the only PM is away, with PM review afterward. Compliant?
cards.back: No. APM advice must be pre-approved by a Portfolio Manager BEFORE it is given. A full PM needs no pre-approval and may also do RR activities.
cards.source_quote: "An Associate Portfolio Manager must not advise on securities unless, before giving the advice, the advice has been pre-approved by a Portfolio Manager." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 2994

cards.front: Client types: RR approved for retail clients vs RR approved for institutional clients — who may each serve?
cards.back: Retail-approved RR: orders/advice for ALL client types. Institutional-approved RR: orders/advice for institutional clients only.
cards.source_quote: "(b) institutional clients, may take orders from, or give advice to, institutional clients only." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 3006

cards.front: TRAP: An RR accepts a gift card from a third-party company for dealer-related referrals. Allowed?
cards.back: No. An Approved Person must not accept any remuneration, gratuity, benefit or other consideration from anyone but the Dealer Member, its related companies, or affiliates.
cards.source_quote: "any remuneration, gratuity, benefit or other consideration from any person other than the Dealer Member, its related companies, or affiliates" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 2931

cards.front: An Approved Person's CIRO approval ceases. What must the former Approved Person do?
cards.back: Immediately cease any activity requiring Corporation approval — no wind-down period, even unpaid 'favours' for former clients.
cards.source_quote: "If an Approved Person ceases to be approved, the former Approved Person must immediately cease any activity requiring Corporation approval." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 2928

cards.front: May the designated Supervisor approve a new account with documents still outstanding, then diarize?
cards.back: No — the designated Supervisor must not approve a new account unless all client account records have been collected.
cards.source_quote: "The designated Supervisor must not approve a new account unless all client account records have been collected." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4641

cards.front: Deadline for designated Supervisor approval of a new account, and the consequence if not given?
cards.back: Approval no later than one business day after the initial trade; otherwise restrict the account to liquidating trades, transfers out, paying out funds or delivering securities.
cards.source_quote: "restrict the account to only liquidating trades, transfers out, paying out funds or delivering securities to the client." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4649

cards.front: Opening an account for an employee of ANOTHER Dealer Member — two required steps?
cards.back: Obtain written approval from the other Dealer Member, and designate the account as a non-client account.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer Member must obtain written approval from the other Dealer Member, and must designate the account as non-client account." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4652

cards.front: Sequencing for a margin account agreement?
cards.back: Prior to opening the margin account: deliver the agreement to the client AND obtain a copy signed by the client.
cards.source_quote: "Prior to opening a margin account, a Dealer Member must: (i) deliver a margin account agreement to the client," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5125

cards.front: Designated Supervisor finds a client's intended derivatives strategies inappropriate. Reject the account outright?
cards.back: No — restrict the account from inappropriate strategies, note trading restrictions on the derivatives account approval, and communicate them to the assigned RR/PM/APM.
cards.source_quote: "restrict the account from using inappropriate strategies and note on the derivatives account approval any trading restrictions imposed" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5183

cards.front: TRAP: Discretionary account agreement — maximum term and renewal? When must it be signed?
cards.back: Maximum 12 months, NOT renewable, and entered with the client prior to accepting the account as discretionary; Supervisor approves account and signed agreement.
cards.source_quote: "(iii) have a maximum term of no longer than 12 months, (iv) not be renewable," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5373

cards.front: Client insists on an action failing suitability. Three conditions before the dealer may carry it out?
cards.back: Inform client of basis for determination and advise against proceeding; recommend an alternative satisfying 3402(1); receive recorded confirmation of the instruction to proceed.
cards.source_quote: "received recorded confirmation of the retail client's instruction to proceed with the action despite the determination" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5591

cards.front: Name the non-trade events that trigger a fresh retail suitability review under 3402(2).
cards.back: Deposit/transfer-in of holdings; new RR/PM/APM designated to the account; known change in client KYC information; known change in a held security; the 3209(4) periodic review.
cards.source_quote: "a Registered Representative, Portfolio Manager or Associate Portfolio Manager is designated as responsible for the account," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5559

cards.front: TRAP: Each security in a recommendation passes suitability individually. Determination done?
cards.back: No — the dealer must also determine the resulting account PORTFOLIO is suitable for the retail client and puts the client's interest first.
cards.source_quote: "the retail client's account portfolio of investments that would result from the investment action the Dealer Member takes, recommends or exercises discretion to take is suitable" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5582

cards.front: Institutional suitability: dealer concludes client CAN independently decide and evaluate risk — vs CANNOT. Consequences?
cards.back: Can: suitability obligation is fulfilled for that transaction. Cannot: dealer must take steps to ensure the client fully understands the product, including potential risks.
cards.source_quote: "capable of making an independent investment decision and independently evaluating the investment risk, then the Dealer Member's suitability obligation is fulfilled for that transaction," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5629

cards.front: How often must the designated Supervisor review each DISCRETIONARY account's financial performance — vs managed accounts?
cards.back: Discretionary: at least monthly, non-delegable, including whether the authorized RR should continue. Managed: quarterly, for objectives pursued and compliant handling.
cards.source_quote: "must also review the financial performance of each discretionary account at least monthly." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8378

cards.front: TRAP: When must a Supervisor review an RR-initiated discretionary order — before or after entry?
cards.back: Default: PRIOR to order entry. Next-business-day review applies only if the RR is approved as a Portfolio Manager or is also an Executive.
cards.source_quote: "A designated Supervisor must review any discretionary order initiated in a discretionary account by a Registered Representative prior to the order being entered unless:" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8386

cards.front: Review cadence for derivatives accounts designated as discretionary and managed accounts?
cards.back: On a daily AND monthly basis, the designated Supervisor must review all such derivatives accounts.
cards.source_quote: "On a daily and monthly basis, the designated Supervisor must review all derivatives accounts that are designated as discretionary accounts and managed accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8314

cards.front: What must OEO trade-review policies specifically address, given suitability review does not apply?
cards.back: The risks of the method of order entry and the absence of intermediation by Dealer Member employees; clients must never receive recommendations.
cards.source_quote: "must specifically address the risks associated with the method of order entry and the absence of intermediation by employees of the Dealer Member." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8267

cards.front: When must relationship disclosure information be provided to a retail client?
cards.back: At the time of opening an account or accounts, and when there is a significant change to the relationship disclosure information previously provided.
cards.source_quote: "(i) at the time of opening an account or accounts, and (ii) when there is a significant change to the relationship disclosure information previously provided to the client." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4695

cards.front: Dealer-level product due diligence: three reasonable steps before making products available?
cards.back: Assess relevant aspects (structure, features, risks, initial/ongoing costs and their impact); approve products to be made available; monitor them for significant changes.
cards.source_quote: "(ii) approve the securities or derivatives to be made available to clients, and (iii) monitor the securities or derivatives for significant changes." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5496

cards.front: KYP split: dealer (3301) vs Approved Person (3302) — who does what?
cards.back: Dealer approves the shelf via product due diligence; the AP must personally understand each product's structure, features, risks and costs — and may only recommend dealer-approved products.
cards.source_quote: "unless the Approved Person takes steps to understand the securities or derivatives, including the securities' or derivatives' structure, features, risks, initial and ongoing costs" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5503

cards.front: TRAP: For an OEO account, which KYP obligation falls away — dealer's 3301 or AP's 3302?
cards.back: Only the AP's KYP (3302) does not apply to OEO accounts; the dealer's product due diligence (3301) still applies.
cards.source_quote: "Section 3302 does not apply in respect to: (i) an order execution only account," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5514

cards.front: Who carries primary responsibility for KYC and suitability compliance, and can it be delegated?
cards.back: The RR, PM or APM assigned to the client account; the responsibility must not be delegated to any other person (KYC 3209; suitability 3406).
cards.source_quote: "Compliance with the Corporation requirements relating to know-your-client is primarily the responsibility of the Registered Representative, Portfolio Manager or Associate Portfolio Manager assigned to the client account." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4545

cards.front: Six client elements KYC must collect sufficiently to enable Rule 3400 suitability obligations?
cards.back: Personal circumstances; financial circumstances; investment needs and objectives; investment knowledge; risk profile; investment time horizon.
cards.source_quote: "ensure it has collected sufficient information regarding all of the following to enable it to meet its obligations under Rule 3400:" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4424

cards.front: Per syllabus 6.6, the fluid nature of a client's circumstances may impact which five things?
cards.back: Finances; investment knowledge; risk profile; investment needs and objectives; time horizon — so clients must be educated to report changes.
cards.source_quote: "Fluid nature of a client's circumstances that may impact their: - Finances - Investment knowledge - Risk profile" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1335

cards.front: Opening an initial account for a TRUST — what identity information must the dealer obtain?
cards.back: The names and addresses of all trustees and all known beneficiaries and settlors of the trust.
cards.source_quote: "in the case of a trust, obtain the names and addresses of all trustees and all known beneficiaries and settlors of the trust," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4457

cards.front: Initial CORPORATE account: deadline for directors' names, and the ownership threshold for identifying individuals?
cards.back: Names of all directors within 30 days of opening; establish identity of any individual beneficially owning or controlling 25% or more of voting rights.
cards.source_quote: "obtain the names of all directors of the corporation within 30 days of opening the account," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4468

cards.front: TRAP: Must relationship disclosure be provided to institutional clients?
cards.back: No — Dealer Members are not required to provide relationship disclosure to institutional clients; section 3216 sets minimums for retail clients only.
cards.source_quote: "Dealer Members are not required to provide relationship disclosure to institutional clients." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4687

cards.front: Beyond passing the prescribed exams, what does the proficiency principle require of every Approved Person?
cards.back: Education, experience and training a reasonable person would consider necessary to perform competently — including understanding the structure, features and risks of each product dealt with, recommended or supervised.
cards.source_quote: "has the education, experience and training that a reasonable person would consider necessary to perform the activity competently," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 3099

cards.front: Minimum experience for an Associate Portfolio Manager applicant?
cards.back: Minimum two years of relevant investment management experience acceptable to the Corporation, within three years prior to the application date. (PM via CIM route: four years.)
cards.source_quote: "Associate Portfolio Manager: Minimum two years of relevant investment management experience acceptable to the Corporation within three years prior to the date of application for approval," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 3161

cards.front: Derivatives qualification: what must the dealer ensure before persons trade/advise in derivatives accounts, and who approves solicitation?
cards.back: Persons trading or advising in derivatives accounts must meet minimum proficiency requirements; the designated Supervisor must approve solicitation of clients to use derivatives programs.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member must ensure that persons trading on its behalf or advising clients in derivatives accounts meet minimum proficiency requirements." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5168

cards.front: Derivatives risk disclosure statement: when delivered, and what records follow?
cards.back: Before accepting an initial derivatives order: provide the most recent Corporation-approved statement, obtain acknowledgement of receipt, provide amendments, and record clients' names, addresses and provision dates.
cards.source_quote: "provide each derivatives retail client with the most recent derivatives risk disclosure statement or other similar document, approved by Corporation before accepting an initial derivatives order" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5286

cards.front: Three acceptable forms of record for a client's OEO disclosure acknowledgement?
cards.back: Client's signature or initials on a form specifically related to the disclosure; an electronic acknowledgement attached to the disclosure text; a tape recording of a verbal acknowledgement.
cards.source_quote: "(ii) an electronic acknowledgement attached to the disclosure and acknowledgement text, or (iii) a tape recording of a verbal acknowledgement." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5026

cards.front: TRAP: what may a pre-2026 grandfathered derivatives AP deal in after the new proficiency model?
cards.back: Only legacy options or futures contracts / futures contract options — and the scope of permitted activities must be clear in all communications and dealings.
cards.source_quote: "may only deal in, as applicable, legacy options or futures contract, futures" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 3334

cards.front: What happens if an Approved Person misses a post-approval proficiency deadline?
cards.back: Automatic suspension by the Corporation; reinstatement once the requirement is completed and the Corporation notified.
cards.source_quote: "The Corporation will automatically suspend an Approved Person if they do not complete the" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 2975

cards.front: Who must approve the relationship disclosure information provided to a client?
cards.back: A partner, Director, officer or designated Supervisor — approval required regardless of format.
cards.source_quote: "The relationship disclosure information provided to the client must be approved by a partner, Director, officer or designated Supervisor. This approval must occur regardless of the form the relationship disclosure information takes." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4834

cards.front: Derivatives clients must be notified of which four matters under 3962(4)?
cards.back: Approaching expiry dates; significant changes in derivatives from changes in the underlying; changes in the dealer's business policy; new trading/regulatory developments impacting clients.
cards.source_quote: "The Dealer Member must have policies and procedures that specifically address notifying clients of: (i) approaching expiry dates," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 8316-8318

cards.front: UMIR 1.1: what is a jitney order?
cards.back: An order entered on a marketplace by a Participant acting for or on behalf of another Participant.
cards.source_quote: "jitney order" means an order entered on a marketplace by a Participant acting for or on behalf of another Participant. — umir.txt ~line 1215

cards.front: UMIR 1.1: limit order vs market order — what price does each execute at?
