cards.back: Limit: buy at a specified maximum price, sell at a specified minimum price. Market: executes upon entry at best ask (buy) or best bid (sell).
cards.source_quote: "limit order" means an order to: (a) buy a security or derivative to be executed at a specified maximum price; or (b) sell a security or derivative to be executed at a specified minimum price. — umir.txt ~line 1306

cards.front: TRAP: Market-on-Close Order vs Closing Price Order — which one participates in SETTING the closing price?
cards.back: Market-on-Close: entered during the day for calculating and executing at the close. Closing Price Order: trades at the already-established close, executed afterward.
cards.source_quote: "Market-on-Close Order" means an order for the purchase or sale of a security entered on a marketplace on a trading day for the purpose of calculating and executing at the closing price — umir.txt ~line 1364

cards.front: UMIR 1.1: what is a Volume-Weighted Average Price Order?
cards.back: An order entered on a marketplace on a trading day to execute trades at an average price of the security traded that day (marketplaces known at entry).
cards.source_quote: means an order for the purchase or sale of a security entered on a marketplace on a trading day for the purpose of executing trades at an average price of the security traded on that trading day — umir.txt ~line 2390

cards.front: TRAP: a client attaches a PRICE condition to an order. Does that make it a Special Terms Order?
cards.back: No. Special Terms Orders are odd lots, conditions OTHER than price/settlement-date/marketplace-imposed, or unusual settlement. Price conditions are just limit orders.
cards.source_quote: (b) the execution of which is subject to a condition other than as: (i) to price, (ii) to the date of settlement; — umir.txt ~line 2238

cards.front: UMIR 1.1: what must a Participant do BEFORE entering a Basis Order?
cards.back: Report to a Market Regulator its intention to enter the order and the details of the related transactions, in the form and manner the regulator requires.
cards.source_quote: provided that prior to the entry of the order the Participant or Access Person reports to a Market Regulator its intention to enter the order and the details of the related transactions — umir.txt ~line 199

cards.front: TRAP: a client says 'you can always trade ahead of my future orders.' Valid consent under client priority (Policy 5.3)?
cards.back: No. Consent must be specific to a particular order, noted on the ticket. Blanket consent for future orders is invalid.
cards.source_quote: A client cannot give a blanket form of consent to permit the Participant to trade alongside or ahead of any future orders the client may give the Participant. — umir.txt ~line 5190

cards.front: UMIR 8.1 client-principal trading: size/value thresholds and price condition for filling a client order from inventory?
cards.back: Client orders of 50 standard trading units or less AND $100,000 or less: execute against principal only at a better price, best available for the client.
cards.source_quote: A Participant that receives a client order for 50 standard trading units or less of a security with a value of $100,000 or less may execute the client order against a principal order or non-client order at a better price — umir.txt ~line 9029

cards.front: IDPC 3121: the four BROAD best execution factors for listed security/derivative client orders?
cards.back: Price of the security/derivative; speed of execution; certainty of execution; overall cost of the transaction when costs are passed on to clients.
cards.source_quote: (i) the price of the security or derivative, (ii) the speed of execution of the client order, (iii) the certainty of execution of the client order, and (iv) the overall cost of the transaction — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4112

cards.front: IDPC 5113(4): when total loan value in a client margin account is NET NEGATIVE, what makes the account in good standing?
cards.back: The cash balance must be a credit equal to or larger than the net negative loan value.
cards.source_quote: If the total loan value in a client account results in a net negative loan value, the cash balance in the margin account must be a credit equal to or larger than the net negative loan value — idpc-rules.txt ~line 12078

cards.front: IDPC 3816: name five items every trade confirmation must contain (besides security-specific details).
cards.back: Day and marketplace(s) of the trade; any securities-regulator fee; salesperson name; agent dealer used; settlement date.
cards.source_quote: the day and the marketplace or marketplaces where the trade took place, or marketplace disclosure language acceptable to the Corporation; the fee or other charge, if any, levied by any securities regulatory authority — idpc-rules.txt ~line 7516

cards.front: UMIR 6.3 order exposure: which client orders must be entered IMMEDIATELY for display, absent an exception?
cards.back: Client orders of 50 standard trading units or less; exceptions include client instructions, better-price execution on receipt, orders over $100,000, and specified order types.
cards.source_quote: A Participant shall immediately enter for display on a marketplace that displays orders in accordance with Part 7 of the Marketplace Operation Instrument a client order to purchase or sell 50 standard trading units or less — umir.txt ~line 5902

cards.front: Desk receives an order to sell at $3 a stock trading at $20. Policy 6.3 says do what?
cards.back: Withhold entry and return the order to the branch for confirmation of terms — the price or symbol is likely wrong.
cards.source_quote: a Participant who receives an order to sell a security at $3 in a stock trading at $20 may return the order to the branch, as it is likely that either the price or the stock symbol is wrong. — umir.txt ~line 5959

cards.front: IDPC 3214(5): the designated Supervisor declines to approve a new account after the initial trade. What must the dealer do?
cards.back: Restrict the account to liquidating trades, transfers out, paying out funds or delivering securities — until the designated Supervisor gives final approval.
cards.source_quote: the Dealer Member must restrict the account to only liquidating trades, transfers out, paying out funds or delivering securities to the client. — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4648

cards.front: TRAP: gatekeeper records (UMIR 10.16(5)) — retention period, and does reporting to the regulator discharge other obligations?
cards.back: Retain not less than SEVEN years from record creation, open to Market Regulator inspection. Reporting is IN ADDITION to securities-legislation, SRO and Marketplace-Rule obligations.
cards.source_quote: retain the records for a period of not less than seven years from the creation of the record; and — umir.txt ~line 10609

cards.front: UMIR 7.1(3): who must each Participant appoint, and to do what?
cards.back: A head of trading, responsible to supervise the trading activities of the Participant in a marketplace.
cards.source_quote: Each Participant shall appoint a head of trading who shall be responsible to supervise the trading activities of the Participant in a marketplace. — umir.txt ~line 6537

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 2: who may conduct the compliance function at a Participant?
cards.back: Persons OTHER than those who supervise the trading activity — supervision and compliance must be separately designated.
cards.source_quote: Designate individuals responsible for supervision and compliance. The compliance function must be conducted by persons other than those who supervise the trading activity. — umir.txt ~line 6750

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 2: how often must the trading supervision SYSTEM itself be reviewed?
cards.back: At least annually; more frequent reviews may be required if past reviews detected problems with supervision and compliance.
cards.source_quote: Review the supervision system at least annually to ensure it continues to be reasonably designed to prevent and detect violations of Requirements. — umir.txt ~line 6772

cards.front: TRAP: how long must results of trading-compliance reviews be kept — five or seven years?
cards.back: FIVE years (Policy 7.1 Part 2). Seven years is the gatekeeper-records and client-withholding-instruction period. Exam swaps them.
cards.source_quote: Maintain results of all reviews for at least five years. — umir.txt ~line 6789

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 2: what goes to the board of directors, and how often?
cards.back: A summary of compliance reviews conducted and the supervision-system review results, at least annually; significant issues must be reported to the board immediately.
cards.source_quote: Report to the board of directors of the Participant or, if applicable, the partners, a summary of the compliance reviews conducted and the results of the supervision system review. These reports must be made at least annually. — umir.txt ~line 6790

cards.front: TRAP: no trading violations occurred, but the Supervisor skipped the firm's prescribed reviews. Compliant?
cards.back: No. A supervisor who has not followed the Participant's supervision procedures fails their Rule 7.1(4) supervisory obligations — independent of any underlying violation.
cards.source_quote: If a supervisor has not followed the supervision procedures adopted by the Participant, the supervisor will have failed to comply with their supervisory obligations under Rule 7.1(4). — umir.txt ~line 6656

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 3 minimum regular-review list: match Frontrunning, Client Priority, Client/Principal Trading to their rule numbers.
cards.back: Frontrunning = Rule 4.1; Client/Principal Trading = Rule 8.1; Client Priority = Rule 5.3. Distractors scramble these pairings.
cards.source_quote: Frontrunning (Rule 4.1) •    Client/Principal Trading (Rule 8.1) •    Client Priority (Rule 5.3) — umir.txt ~line 6827

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 10 audit trail: name five data points that must be accurately recorded per order/trade.
cards.back: Date/time of entry, amendment, cancellation, execution, expiration; quantity; buy/sell/short marker; market-or-limit marker; price if limit. (Plus security, trader, client, consent, designations.)
cards.source_quote: date and time of entry, amendment, cancellation, execution and expiration •    quantity •    buy, sell or short-sale marker •    market or limit order marker •    price (if limit order) — umir.txt ~line 7095

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 12: how often must trading in Grey/Watch list and Restricted list securities be reviewed?
cards.back: Daily. (Contrast: manipulative/deceptive sampling, audit trail, order handling and trade confirmations are quarterly.)
cards.source_quote: Each Participant must review the trading described above on a daily basis. — umir.txt ~line 7146

cards.front: UMIR 10.14: to whose clock must Participants synchronize the clocks used for recording UMIR events?
cards.back: The clock used by the Market Regulator for this purpose — not the TSX, a national time standard, or the clearing agency.
cards.source_quote: Each marketplace and each Participant shall synchronize the clocks used for recording the time and date of any event that must be recorded pursuant to UMIR to the clock used by the Market Regulator — umir.txt ~line 10443

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 3: review frequency and sample size must be commensurate with which seven risk factors?
cards.back: Participant's size (revenue, market share, exposure, trade volume); organizational structure; number/location of offices; product/service complexity; registrants per location; disciplinary history; risk profile/red flags.
cards.source_quote: the Participant's size (considering factors such as revenue, market share, market exposure and volume of trades) •    the Participant's organizational structure •    number and location of the Participant's offices — umir.txt ~line 6840

cards.front: TRAP: which is NOT a Policy 7.1 Part 3 risk-based supervision factor — disciplinary history of registrants, or the Supervisor's personal performance?
cards.back: The Supervisor's personal performance. The listed factor is the disciplinary history of registered representatives or associated persons; personal performance appears nowhere on the list.
cards.source_quote: the disciplinary history of registered representatives or associated persons •    the risk profile of the Participant's business and any indicators of irregularities or misconduct i.e. "red flags". — umir.txt ~line 6847

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 9: which non-institutional investors can qualify for direct electronic access?
cards.back: Almost none — precluded except in exceptional circumstances, generally sophisticated former traders and floor brokers, or persons with assets approaching an institutional investor's.
cards.source_quote: non-institutional investors will be precluded from qualifying for direct electronic access except in exceptional circumstances generally limited to sophisticated former traders and floor brokers — umir.txt ~line 7031

cards.front: UMIR 10.16 gatekeeper flow: an employee spots possible manipulative account activity. Trace inquiry → document → report.
cards.back: Employee forthwith reports to supervisor/compliance; review; if a violation may exist: written records, diligent investigation, written findings, report to Market Regulator by the 15th of the following month.
cards.source_quote: An officer, director, partner or employee of a Participant shall forthwith report to their supervisor or the compliance department of the Participant upon becoming aware of activity in a principal account, non-client account or client account — umir.txt ~line 10552

cards.front: TRAP: frontrunning (UMIR 4.1) vs insider trading — what knowledge triggers the 4.1 prohibition, and what conduct is caught?
cards.back: Knowledge of an UNENTERED CLIENT ORDER reasonably expected to move price — not inside issuer information. Caught: principal/non-client orders (e.g., personal account ahead of the client), soliciting, tipping.
cards.source_quote: A Participant with knowledge of a client order that on entry could reasonably be expected to affect the market price of a security or a derivative, shall not, prior to the entry of such client order, — umir.txt ~line 4614

cards.front: UMIR 1.1: when does an Opening Order CEASE to be an Opening Order?
cards.back: If the order does not trade at the opening of trading of that security on that marketplace on that trading day.
cards.source_quote: "provided an order shall cease to be an Opening Order if the order does not trade at the opening of trading of that security on that marketplace on that trading day" — umir.txt ~line 1636

cards.front: UMIR 7.11: a marketplace varies an executed trade BEFORE settlement at a party's request. What two conditions apply?
cards.back: Consent of each Participant and Access Person that is a party to the trade, plus immediate notice to the Market Regulator in its required form and manner.
cards.source_quote: "the marketplace on which the trade was executed at the request of a party to the trade and with the consent of each Participant and Access Person that is a party to the trade" — umir.txt ~line 8738

cards.front: TRAP: name the UMIR 4.1(2) carve-outs — when is trading ahead of a known client order NOT frontrunning?
cards.back: Decision-makers had no actual knowledge; for that client's benefit; solicited to facilitate the client order; ordinary-practice hedge of a pre-existing position; pre-existing legal obligation; arbitrage account.
cards.source_quote: "no director, officer, partner, employee or agent of the Participant who made or participated in making the decision to enter a principal order or non-client order or to solicit an order had actual knowledge of the client order" — umir.txt ~line 4628

cards.front: UMIR 2.1(2)(b): what trading pattern is a specific unacceptable activity when a client has expressed interest in a security?
cards.back: Making a pattern of trading in that particular security with knowledge of the client's expression of interest in it.
cards.source_quote: "make a pattern of trading in a particular security with knowledge of an expression of interest by a client in that particular security" — umir.txt ~line 2883

cards.front: IDPC 3221(2): when does the discretionary-trading prohibition NOT apply to time and price discretion?
cards.back: When exercised in fulfilling the dealer's best execution obligation relating to a client order for a specific amount or a specific security.
cards.source_quote: "Subsection 3221(1) does not apply to time and price discretion exercised in fulfilling the Dealer Member’s best execution obligation relating to a client order for a specific amount or a specific security." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4921

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 7: what must automated pre-entry controls for electronic access prevent an order from exceeding?
cards.back: Pre-determined credit or capital thresholds of the Participant/Access Person; client credit or other assigned limits; pre-determined value/volume limits on unexecuted orders per security or class.
cards.source_quote: "automated controls to examine each order before entry on a marketplace to prevent the entry of an order which would result in: o the Participant or Access Person exceeding pre-determined credit or capital thresholds" — umir.txt ~line 6944

cards.front: Policy 7.1 Part 5: WHEN are artificial-price orders more likely to appear?
cards.back: At the end of a month, quarter or year, or on the expiry date of options on a listed security.
cards.source_quote: "orders which are intended to or which affect an artificial price are more likely to appear at the end of a month, quarter or year or on the date of the expiry of options" — umir.txt ~line 6890

cards.front: What two things must a dealer's public-communications framework include under IDPC 3601(1)?
cards.back: Policies and procedures specifically addressing communication with the public, plus monitoring of compliance with them to give reasonable assurance the dealer, employees and Approved Persons comply.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member’s policies and procedures must specifically address communication with the public and the Dealer Member must monitor compliance with these policies and procedures" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5839-5840

cards.front: List the seven content standards a communication must not breach under IDPC 3602(1).
cards.back: Untrue statement/omission of material fact; unjustified promise of specific results; unrepresentative statistics/unidentified assumptions; unlabeled opinion or forecast; unfair risk presentation; detrimental to public/Corporation/dealers; non-compliant with requirements/laws.
cards.source_quote: "contains an untrue statement or omission of a material fact or is otherwise false or misleading, (ii) contains an unjustified promise of specific results," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5855-5857

cards.front: TRAP: 'My portfolio returned 22% — join and lock in the same results!' The return figure is accurate. Compliant?
cards.back: No. Accuracy of past performance doesn't cure an unjustified promise of specific results — prohibited content under 3602(1)(ii).
cards.source_quote: "contains an unjustified promise of specific results," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5857

cards.front: TRAP: a flyer is 100% factually accurate but describes only the upside of a strategy. Which 3602(1) standard is breached?
cards.back: Fails to fairly present the potential risks to the client — 3602(1)(v). Accuracy does not cure one-sided presentation; distinct from the untrue-statement clause (i).
cards.source_quote: "fails to fairly present the potential risks to the client," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5861

cards.front: What does the misleading-communications rule 3640(1) prohibit, including via trade names?
cards.back: Holding out in a manner reasonably expected to deceive or mislead as to proficiency/experience/qualifications/registration category, the relationship with dealer or Approved Person, or products/services provided.
cards.source_quote: "including through the use of a trade name, in a manner that could reasonably be expected to deceive or mislead any person or company" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6098-6099

cards.front: Which three title usages are banned for client-facing Approved Persons under 3640(2) (think 'top producer' awards, ranking lists)?
cards.back: Title/designation/award/recognition based partly or entirely on sales activity or revenue generation; corporate officer title without appointment under corporate law; any title/designation the dealer hasn't approved.
cards.source_quote: "if based partly or entirely on that Approved Person’s sales activity or revenue generation, a title, designation, award, or recognition," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6109-6110

cards.front: Material NOT on the 3602(3) pre-approval list must be reviewed how?
cards.back: In a manner appropriate to the type of material: pre-use approval, post-use review, or post-use sampling — 3602(4).
cards.source_quote: "is reviewed in a manner appropriate to the type of material through: (i) pre-use approval, (ii) post-use review, or (iii) post-use sampling." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5880-5883

cards.front: How long, and in what form, must advertisements, sales literature, correspondence and supervision records be retained?
cards.back: Minimum seven years from record creation, in durable and accessible form (3803); copies must be readily available for inspection by the Corporation (3602(6)).
cards.source_quote: "in durable and accessible form, for a minimum of seven years from the date the record is created" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6721-6722

cards.front: Which legislative requirement and which channel risk does syllabus 8.1 single out for communications supervision?
cards.back: Canadian Anti-spam Legislation (CASL); off-channel communication issues — business messages outside firm systems escape required review, supervision and retention.
cards.source_quote: "Legislative requirements, including Canadian Anti-spam Legislation • Off-channel communication issues" — syllabus-raw.txt ~lines 1531-1532

cards.front: Name the six items a designated Supervisor must approve BEFORE use or publication (3602(3)).
cards.back: Research reports; market letters; telemarketing scripts; promotional seminar texts (excluding educational seminar texts); original advertisements or original template advertisements; any material containing performance reports/summaries used to solicit clients.
cards.source_quote: "approved by a designated Supervisor before use or publication: (i) research reports, (ii) market letters, (iii) telemarketing scripts," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5866-5870

cards.front: TRAP: do ALL seminar texts require pre-use approval by a designated Supervisor?
cards.back: No — promotional seminar texts do; educational seminar texts are expressly excluded from the pre-approval list (still reviewable under 3602(4) methods).
cards.source_quote: "promotional seminar texts (excluding educational seminar texts)," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5871

cards.front: New ad template vs branch-populated copies — what exactly needs designated-Supervisor pre-approval?
cards.back: The original advertisement or original template advertisement. Routine populated copies of an approved template are not separately listed; they fall under the 3602(4) review methods.
cards.source_quote: "original advertisements or original template advertisements, and" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5872

cards.front: A weekly 'market pulse' email with sector recommendations — what approval does each issue need?
cards.back: Each issue is a market letter: designated-Supervisor approval before use or publication. Next-morning review is post-use review, unavailable for listed items.
cards.source_quote: "approved by a designated Supervisor before use or publication: (i) research reports, (ii) market letters," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5866-5869

cards.front: TRAP: a 'meeting handout' shows model-portfolio returns and is given to prospects. Exempt from pre-approval?
cards.back: No. Any material containing performance reports or summaries used to solicit clients requires designated-Supervisor approval before use — the label is irrelevant; content plus solicitation use triggers it.
cards.source_quote: "any material containing performance reports or summaries that is used to solicit clients." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5873

cards.front: Per the Rule 1201 definition, a 'designated Supervisor' for communications is responsible for what two Rule 3600 roles?
cards.back: Pre-approval of advertising, sales literature and correspondence under Part A, and supervision of research reports under Part B — supervisory roles the dealer makes a Supervisor responsible for.
cards.source_quote: "the pre-approval of advertising, sales literature and correspondence under Part A of Rule 3600, and (vii) the supervision of research reports under Part B of Rule 3600." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 329-331

cards.front: TRAP: a non-designated marketing head approves an original ad; it runs; Monday review finds the content compliant. Any breach?
cards.back: Yes — listed items must be approved by a designated Supervisor before use or publication. Compliant content never cures wrong approver plus late review.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member must ensure that the following items are approved by a designated Supervisor before use or publication:" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5866-5867

cards.front: An in-house fund promo piece with since-inception returns — which two rule hooks bind the dealer before use?
cards.back: Pre-approval as performance material used to solicit clients (3602(3)(vi)); and the piece must not fail to comply with Corporation requirements or any applicable laws (3602(1)(vii)).
cards.source_quote: "fails to comply with Corporation requirements or any applicable laws." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5863

cards.front: At what beneficial-ownership level must a research report disclose the dealer's/affiliates' position in the subject issuer, measured when?
cards.back: One percent or more of any class of the issuer's equity securities, measured as of the end of the month prior to the report's issuance date.
cards.source_quote: "beneficial ownership of the equity securities of the subject issuer that amounts to one percent or more of any class of such securities:" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5929-5930

cards.front: TRAP: report issued April 6 — which month-end governs the 1% ownership measurement?
cards.back: End of February: when issuance is less than 10 days after the prior month-end, measure as of the end of the second most recent month, not March 31.
cards.source_quote: "as of the end of the second most recent month if the report issuance date is less than 10 days after the end of the prior month," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5932-5933

cards.front: Whose long or short positions in the issuer's securities must a research report disclose under 3608(2)(ii)?
cards.back: The analyst; an associate of the analyst; any person directly involved in preparing the report — if they hold or are short any issuer securities, directly or indirectly.
cards.source_quote: "any person directly involved in the preparation of the report, holds or is short any of the issuer’s securities directly or indirectly," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5937-5938

cards.front: What is the look-back window for disclosing investment banking services the dealer provided to the issuer for remuneration?
cards.back: The 12 months immediately preceding the date the research report or recommendation was issued; non-routine paid services by partners/Directors/officers/analysts carry the same 12-month window.
cards.source_quote: "any investment banking services provided by the Dealer Member to the issuer for remuneration during the 12 months immediately preceding the date a research report or recommendation was issued," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5944-5946

cards.front: Routine trade execution for the issuer vs acting as its placement agent — which is disclosable in research?
cards.back: Placement agent work — it is within the 'investment banking' definition. Normal course investment advisory or trade execution services are carved out of the services disclosure.
cards.source_quote: "other than services provided in the normal course investment advisory or trade execution services to the issuer for remuneration," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5940-5941

cards.front: Market-making disclosure: what counts as an 'equity related security' of the subject issuer?
cards.back: A security whose performance is based on an underlying equity security or basket of income-producing assets — derivatives, convertibles, income trust units. Market making in any must be disclosed.
cards.source_quote: "A security whose performance is based on the performance of an underlying equity security or a basket of income producing assets, including derivatives, convertible securities and income trust units." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5901-5903

cards.front: Beyond conflicts, what must every research report disclose (or point to), and what is disclosed quarterly (3609)?
cards.back: Per report: the rating system and how each recommendation fits; the dissemination policies and procedures. Quarterly: the percentage of recommendations in each category of the system.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member must, on a quarterly basis, disclose the percentage of its recommendations that fall into each category of its recommendation system." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5958-5959

cards.front: TRAP: one identical 'conflicts may exist' paragraph in every research report — acceptable under 3610?
cards.back: No. Disclosures must be clear, meaningful, comprehensive and prominent; standard statements are barred when specific information and customized disclosures are more appropriate.
cards.source_quote: "The Dealer Member must not use standard disclosure statements when it is more appropriate to use specific information and customized disclosures" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5967-5968

cards.front: TRAP: coverage is suspended solely because the issuer went on the restricted list. Is discontinuance notice required?
cards.back: No — that is the express exception. Otherwise, notice of intention to suspend/discontinue must go to the same audience, in the same manner, as the coverage.
cards.source_quote: "Notice of discontinuance of coverage is not required if the sole reason for the suspension is that the issuer has been placed on a Dealer Member’s restricted list." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6010-6011

cards.front: A dealer's research policies and procedures must specifically address which three subjects (3607(1))?
cards.back: The conduct of analysts; the publishing of research reports; the making of recommendations by analysts.
cards.source_quote: "(i)     the conduct of analysts, (ii)    the publishing of research reports, and (iii)   the making of recommendations by analysts." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5912-5914

cards.front: TRAP: may a dealer publish research on its own securities or its parent holding company's securities?
cards.back: No — syllabus 8.4 lists the Investment Dealer's own securities or those of its holding company first among the prohibitions on issuing research reports.
cards.source_quote: "Prohibitions on issuing research reports - Investment Dealer’s own securities or those of its holding company" — syllabus-raw.txt ~lines 1549-1550

cards.front: Under 3614, whose relationships with the issuer bar the dealer from issuing the analyst's report, and which relationships?
cards.back: The analyst, an associate of the analyst, or the designated Supervisor — serving as officer, director or employee of the issuer, or in any advisory capacity to it.
cards.source_quote: "the analyst, an associate of the analyst or the designated Supervisor: (i) serves as an officer, director or employee of the issuer, or" — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 5997-5998

cards.front: Quiet periods after acting as manager or co-manager: how long before research on the issuer's equity securities may be issued?
cards.back: 10 days after an initial public offering; three days after a secondary offering. Reports on significant news or a significant event remain permitted within those periods.
cards.source_quote: "for 10 days after the date of the offering of an initial public offering of equity securities of the subject issuer," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6074-6075

cards.front: Name the four prohibited inducements for receiving issuer business or compensation (3617).
cards.back: Offering a favourable research report; offering a favourable rating or price target; offering to delay changing a rating/target/other report element (including issue date); threatening to change a rating/target/element.
cards.source_quote: "offer to delay the changing of a rating or price target on one or more of the issuer’s securities or the changing of any other research report element," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6020-6021

cards.front: Trading blackout for anyone directly involved in preparing or approving a research report: which window, and who can override it?
cards.back: No trading in the subject issuer's equity/equity-related securities from 30 days before until five days after issuance; exception only with prior written approval of a designated Executive.
cards.source_quote: "must not trade in equity securities or equity related securities of the subject issuer for a period beginning 30 days prior to and ending five days after the issuance of the research report." — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6038-6040

cards.front: Syllabus 8.4: what are the three controls for wall-crossing an Analyst?
cards.back: Policies and procedures on detecting and restricting trades; restrictions on relevant individuals; approval by designated Executive. (Executive approval may not bless trades contrary to the recommendation absent special circumstances.)
cards.source_quote: "Controls for wall-crossing an Analyst - Policies and procedures on detecting and restricting trades - Restrictions on relevant individuals - Approval by designated Executive" — syllabus-raw.txt ~lines 1596-1599

cards.front: Research independence from investment banking: what four minimums must the policies address (3621(2))?
cards.back: Prohibit banking-department approval of research; limit banking's involvement solely to correcting factual errors; prohibit/prevent banking's advance notice of new or changed ratings; systems controlling and recording analyst-banking information flow.
cards.source_quote: "prohibiting the approval of research reports by the investment banking department, (ii) limiting the investment banking department’s involvement in the production of research reports solely to the correction of factual errors," — idpc-rules.txt ~lines 6063-6065

cards.front: What must happen before an analyst takes on any outside activity (3623)?
cards.back: The Dealer Member must pre-approve the analyst's outside activities — approval comes first, regardless of whether the engagement involves a private company or a covered name.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member must pre-approve an analyst’s outside activities." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 6085

cards.front: IDPC 3608(2)(v): whose NAME must a research report disclose when dealer personnel overlap with the issuer?
cards.back: Any partner, Director, officer, employee or agent of the dealer who is a partner, director, officer or employee of the issuer, or serves it in an equivalent advisory capacity.
cards.source_quote: "the name of any partner, Director, officer, employee or agent of the Dealer Member who is a partner, director, officer or employee of the issuer, or who serves in an equivalent advisory capacity to the issuer" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5947

cards.front: TRAP: a dealer distributes an independent third party's research under THAT party's name. Do 3608/3609 disclosures apply?
cards.back: Yes by default (3611(1)). Exempt only for FINRA/approved-regulator reports, or access-only/client-requested supply — and the dealer must disclose it wasn't prepared to Canadian requirements.
cards.source_quote: "The disclosures required by sections 3608 and 3609 are applicable to research reports prepared by an independent third party that is distributed by a Dealer Member to its clients under the independent third party’s name." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5971

cards.front: IDPC 3620(1): which analyst-compensation fact must a research report disclose?
cards.back: Whether the responsible analyst received compensation within the prior 12 months based upon the dealer's investment banking revenues.
cards.source_quote: "A research report must disclose if the analyst responsible for the report received compensation within the prior 12 months that was based upon the Dealer Member’s investment banking revenues." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 6053

cards.front: IDPC 3613: what two issuer-visit facts must research reports disclose?
cards.back: Whether, and to what extent, the analyst visited the issuer's material operations; and whether the issuer paid or reimbursed any of the analyst's travel expenses for the visit.
cards.source_quote: "whether, and to what extent, an analyst has visited the issuer’s material operations, and (ii) if the issuer has paid or reimbursed any of the analyst’s travel expenses with respect to the visit." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5992

cards.front: IDPC 3618: an employee gives a TV interview about an issuer's merits. What must they disclose?
cards.back: Whether or not the Dealer Member has issued a relevant research report — applies to interviews and any public comment on an issuer or its securities.
cards.source_quote: "an employee or Approved Person of a Dealer Member must disclose whether or not the Dealer Member has issued a relevant research report." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 6027

cards.front: What are the five components of business location supervision guidelines?
cards.back: Scope; planning; business location audit program and related training; risk identification; audit report and follow-up.
cards.source_quote: "Understand the guidelines for business location supervision, including: • Scope • Planning • Business location audit program and training • Risk identification • Audit report and follow up" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1612

cards.front: An audit finds deficiencies at a branch. What must happen after the audit report?
cards.back: Documented follow-up: findings tracked to corrective action — unresolved findings escalate; they are never withheld from Executives.
cards.source_quote: "Inadequate follow-up procedures to ensure that compliance audit findings are properly tracked and corrective action is taken by a business location" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1652

cards.front: How long must supervision records (e.g., records of branch reviews) be retained?
cards.back: Minimum 7 years from the date the record is created, in durable and accessible form.
cards.source_quote: "in durable and accessible form, for a minimum of seven years from the date the" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 6721

cards.front: Who oversees the Supervisors at a dealer's business locations?
cards.back: Head office: the dealer must supervise its Supervisors (IDPC 3905 head-office oversight), supported by a location audit program.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member must have procedures in place that ensure that Supervisors are properly performing their supervisory functions." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 7935

cards.front: Name the five business-location risk-factor categories in syllabus 9.2.
cards.back: Internal; reputational; business-activity; location size; client-base.
cards.source_quote: "Internal risk factors, including: - Adequacy of resources - Key person reliance - Principal-agent • Reputational risk, including: - Community-specific marketing strategy and sales practices - Number of outside activities - Any internal disciplinary action - Number of complaints" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1621

cards.front: Internal risk factors of a business location include which three items?
cards.back: Adequacy of resources; key-person reliance; principal-agent relationship.
cards.source_quote: "Internal risk factors, including: - Adequacy of resources - Key person reliance - Principal-agent" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1621

cards.front: Reputational risk factors of a business location include which four items?
cards.back: Community-specific marketing strategy and sales practices; number of outside activities; internal disciplinary actions; number of complaints.
cards.source_quote: "Reputational risk, including: - Community-specific marketing strategy and sales practices - Number of outside activities - Any internal disciplinary action - Number of complaints" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1625

cards.front: Business-activity risk at a location is driven by which factors?
cards.back: Exposure inherent to a product/service offered there; the location's lines of business and aggregate portfolio risk; number of trade errors.
cards.source_quote: "Business location’s business activity risk, including: - Exposure inherent to providing a particular product or service at a business location - Business location’s lines of business and the aggregate risk present in its overall portfolio - Number of trading errors" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1630

cards.front: Business location SIZE risk includes which pair of considerations?
cards.back: Risk-weighting; risk assessment and control practices (plus registrant count context).
cards.source_quote: "Business location size risk, including: - Risk-weighting - Risk assessment and control practices" — syllabus-raw.txt lines 1663-1665

cards.front: What is client-base risk at a business location?
cards.back: The risk the location assumes that clients may use it for inappropriate purposes.
cards.source_quote: "Client base risk, business location assumes the risk that clients may use it for inappropriate actions" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1638

cards.front: TRAP: 'Number of complaints' belongs to which 9.2 risk category — internal or reputational?
cards.back: Reputational. Internal is resources/key-person/principal-agent. Exam distractors swap items across the five categories.
cards.source_quote: "Reputational risk, including: - Community-specific marketing strategy and sales practices - Number of outside activities - Any internal disciplinary action - Number of complaints" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1625

cards.front: TRAP: 'Number of trade errors' belongs to which 9.2 risk category?
cards.back: Business-activity risk — not size risk. Size risk is risk-weighting and risk assessment/control practices.
cards.source_quote: "Business location’s business activity risk, including: - Exposure inherent to providing a particular product or service at a business location - Business location’s lines of business and the aggregate risk present in its overall portfolio - Number of trading errors" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1630

cards.front: Syllabus 9.3 retail distribution risks — name the product-related ones.
cards.back: Sale of complex or opaque products (incl. illiquid/alternative); new or complex products retail investors don't understand.
cards.source_quote: "The sale of complex or opaque products by individual registrants, including illiquid or alternative products" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1643

cards.front: Which books-and-records failure does 9.3 single out for particular attention?
cards.back: Inadequate control over books and records, with particular attention to poor control of client address changes.
cards.source_quote: "Inadequate control over books and records, with particular attention to poor control of client address changes" — syllabus-raw.txt (Element 9, 9.3 parameters)

cards.front: Which supervision-and-control failures does 9.3 flag for retail distribution?
cards.back: Inadequate control/review of advertising and sales literature; lack of documented evidence of overall supervision.
cards.source_quote: "Failure to meet regulatory standards for supervision and control, including inadequate control and review of advertising and sales literature and the lack of documented evidence of overall supervision" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1647

cards.front: Beyond products and supervision controls, list four more 9.3 retail distribution risks.
cards.back: Client margin management failures; weak follow-up on compliance audit findings; improper reassignment of terminated registrants' accounts; commission-based compensation conflicts.
cards.source_quote: "Failure to meet requirements for managing the margin activities of clients • Inadequate follow-up procedures to ensure that compliance audit findings are properly tracked and corrective action is taken by a business location" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1650

cards.front: A branch repeatedly ignores compliance audit findings. What does 9.3 require?
cards.back: Adequate follow-up procedures: findings tracked and corrective action taken by the business location — non-remediation is itself the named risk.
cards.source_quote: "Inadequate follow-up procedures to ensure that compliance audit findings are properly tracked and corrective action is taken by a business location" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1652

cards.front: TRAP: an AP fails 'regulatory, contractual and legal obligations and the duty of integrity.' Which element/outcome tests this?
cards.back: 9.3 (retail distribution risk framing) — distinct from Element 1 ethics outcomes, which test the conduct standards themselves.
cards.source_quote: "Approved Person’s failure to meet regulatory, contractual and legal obligations and to apply high levels of integrity" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1656

cards.front: Which factors make up a business location's risk profile for supervision purposes?
cards.back: Supervisor experience; personnel tenure/turnover; disciplinary/regulatory history; revenue sources; number of client accounts; share of revenue from higher-risk investments; prior compliance audit findings.
cards.source_quote: "consider the risk profile(s) of relevant business locations (Supervisor experience level; tenure/turnover of personnel; internal/external disciplinary history or regulatory actions; revenue sources; number of client accounts; percentage of revenue from higher-risk investments" — research/02 Part 4, Appendix 16 C3-VIII

cards.front: Who must manage and control the specific risks identified at business locations?
cards.back: Qualified individuals with sufficient resources, assigned to those risks — with red flags identified, followed up and escalated.
cards.source_quote: "ensure management and control of specific risks are assigned to qualified individuals with sufficient resources; identify red flags, follow up and escalate" — 02-syllabus-study-guide-competency-profile.md ~line 349

cards.front: TRAP: does a principal-agent arrangement reduce the dealer's supervisory obligations at that location?
cards.back: No — obligations are unchanged; the arrangement is itself an internal risk factor to supervise (see also Rule 2300).
cards.source_quote: "The Dealer Member must treat each of its agents as employees with respect to:" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 1727

cards.front: What belongs in the business location audit program besides the audits themselves?
cards.back: Related training for those conducting audits, defined scope, and planning.
cards.source_quote: "Scope • Planning • Business location audit program and training • Risk identification • Audit report and follow up" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1613

cards.front: Advisory services vs fully discretionary managed products under 9.3 — why are both named?
cards.back: Both are retail distribution channels whose risks (books/records control, supervision standards, margin, documentation) the Supervisor must apply requirements to.
cards.source_quote: "Apply supervisory requirements to retail distribution risks, including advisory services and fully discretionary managed products to specific situations relevant to a Supervisor." — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1640

cards.front: IDPC 1201: when does a RESIDENCE count as a 'business location'?
cards.back: When regular and ongoing activity requiring registration or approval is carried out from it — or when records relating to such activity are kept there.
cards.source_quote: "an activity that requires registration or Corporation approval is carried out by or on behalf of a Dealer Member, and includes a residence if regular and ongoing activity that requires registration or approval is carried out from the residence" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 232

cards.front: IDPC 2202: what must a dealer do when it opens or closes a business location?
cards.back: Notify the Corporation of the opening or closing, under sub-clause 2803(2)(i)(g) — an NRD submission within the time period prescribed by NI 33-109.
cards.source_quote: "Under sub-clause 2803(2)(i)(g), a Dealer Member must notify the Corporation of the opening or closing of a business location." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 1305

cards.front: Which NRD form notifies a change of address, type of location, or supervision of a business location?
cards.back: Form 33-109F3 — Business locations other than head office (same form used for opening/closing notifications under section 2202).
cards.source_quote: "a notification of change of address, type of Form 33-109F3 - Business locations other than location or supervision of any business head office location" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 3784 (two-column table row, flattened)

cards.front: Rule 2200 Part D: what makes a business location a branch office MEMBER of a CIRO Region?
cards.back: Being in a Region with a Supervisor who is normally present at that business location — every such location is a branch office member of the Region.
cards.source_quote: "Every Dealer Member’s business location in a Region with a Supervisor, who is normally present at the business location, is a branch office member of the Region." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 1612

cards.front: GN-3900-20-001: what is the stated DESIGN purpose of second-tier supervision?
cards.back: Ensure no serious account issues were missed by first-tier supervision, and ensure first-tier supervision is conducted adequately. (First tier: resident Supervisors at their location; second tier: normally head office.)
cards.source_quote: "Dealers should design their second-tier supervision to: ensure no serious account issues were missed by ﬁrst-tier supervision, and ensure ﬁrst-tier supervision is conducted adequately." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 263

cards.front: GN-3900-20-001: second-tier reviewers spot issues they have no authority to resolve. What must exist?
cards.back: Procedures for referring those issues to a Supervisor who has the appropriate authority.
cards.source_quote: "Where the second-tier reviews are conducted by personnel who do not have the authority to resolve the issues they identify, the Dealer should have procedures for referring these issues to a Supervisor who has the appropriate authority." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 269

cards.front: IDPC 3219(3): who must control and investigate returned retail-client mail?
cards.back: A person independent of the sales function (may sit within a business location); a record of all investigations and results must be maintained.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member’s returned mail procedures for retail clients must at a minimum include the following provisions: (i) a rule requiring the control and investigation by a person independent of the sales function" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4895
